A&A: Alterations and Additions — works to existing buildings under BO approval or MWCS
AAB: Antiquities Advisory Board — heritage grading and conservation advice
AMO: Antiquities and Monuments Office — graded buildings and monument protection
AP: Authorized Person registered under Buildings Ordinance Cap. 123 — signs statutory submissions
ArchSD: Architectural Services Department — government works architect (public sector)
AVA: Application for Variation — planning or lease parameter variation (PlanD / LandsD)
BA12: BD form — application for occupation permit (part of OP submission chain)
BA13: BD form — certificate of completion of building works
BA14: BD form — certificate that building is fit for occupation
BD: Buildings Department — building plan approval, consent, occupation permit
BEAM Plus: Hong Kong green building rating tool (NB, EB, Interiors, Neighbourhood)
BHR: Building Height Restriction in metres above Principal Datum or by storeys
BIM: Building Information Modelling — coordinated digital design and issue workflow
BMO: Building Management Ordinance Cap. 344 — management and repair of common parts
BO: Buildings Ordinance Cap. 123 — primary building control legislation
BO s.14: Consent to commence building works and carry out building works
BoQ: Bills of Quantities — tender pricing and measurement document
CA: Contract Administrator — employer's agent under building contract (HKIA forms)
CC: Certificate of Compliance — Lands Department clearance for private land disposals
CDM: Construction design and management coordination — H&S duties on projects
CE: Compensation Event — NEC terminology for change, delay, or risk events
CEDD: Civil Engineering and Development Department — slopes, site formation, and public works interfaces
CLP: CLP Power — electricity supply undertaker (Kowloon and New Territories)
CNP: Construction Noise Permit — EPD approval under Noise Control Ordinance Cap. 400 for prescribed construction activities
D&B: Design and Build procurement — contractor-led design and construction
DEVB: Development Bureau — policy for public works and NEC adoption
DIA: Drainage Impact Assessment — DSD consultation for development drainage effects
DfMA: Design for Manufacture and Assembly — design approach supporting MiC
DLO: District Lands Office — LandsD front door for site conditions and grant compliance
DLP: Defects Liability Period — post-practical-completion contractual remedy period
ELS: Excavation and Lateral Support — basement retention (pile wall, diaphragm wall, strutting)
first fix: MEP sleeves and conduits embedded before concrete pour — reduces chasing
follow-the-structure: Facade works start when superstructure is several floors below top — shortens programme
ITP: Inspection and Test Plan — contractor hold points aligned with QA and AP/RSE witness
second fix: MEP installation after partitions/boarding — follows structure first fix
工序穿插: Overlapping construction trades (fast-tracking) — programme logic, not a HK statutory term
DMBA: Design Manual: Barrier Free Access 2008 — accessibility design standard
EMSD: Electrical and Mechanical Services Department — lifts, escalators, BEC, Form 5
EOT: Extension of Time — contractual relief for qualifying delay events
EPD: Environmental Protection Department — noise, waste, environmental permits
ER: Employer's Requirements — design-build specification package for D&B contracts
FEHD: Food and Environmental Hygiene Department — F&B licensing and layout constraints
FRC: Fire Resisting Construction — compartment walls/floors per FS Code
FS Code: Code of Practice for Fire Safety in Buildings 2011
FSD: Fire Services Department — fire services installations and Fire Certificate
FSI: Fire Services Installation — sprinklers, hydrants, hose reels, alarms, etc.
GBP: General Building Plans — coordinated statutory submission to BD
GCC: General Conditions of Contract — government building works contract terms
GEO: Geotechnical Engineering Office — slope stability and geotechnical standards under CEDD
GFA: Gross Floor Area per BO definition; subject to PNAP APP-2 exemptions
GS: General Specification — technical specification for government/institutional works
HA: Hong Kong Housing Authority — public rental and subsidized housing developer
HD: Housing Department — HA executive arm; Harmony block standards
HIA: Heritage Impact Assessment — required for graded sites and sensitive proposals
HKIA: Hong Kong Institute of Architects — professional body; publishes SFBC
HKICM: Hong Kong Institute of Construction Managers — construction management profession
HKIS: Hong Kong Institute of Surveyors — quantity surveying and property professions
HKPSG: Hong Kong Planning Standards and Guidelines — PlanD development standards
HKWC: Hong Kong Wind Code — wind actions for structural and facade design
HKE: Hongkong Electric — electricity supply undertaker (Hong Kong Island and Lamma)
HyD: Highways Department — roads, slopes interfaces, highway-related submissions
IC: Interim Certificate — periodic payment certificate under building contract
LandsD: Lands Department — lease conditions, modifications, CC, Consent to Assign
LD: Labour Department — occupational safety and health enforcement on sites
MiC: Modular Integrated Construction — pre-fabricated volumetric modules
MLP: Master Layout Plan — lease plan showing building footprint and setbacks
MOD: Modification of Lease — LandsD application to vary lease conditions
MOE: Means of Escape — exit routes, travel distance, exit widths (BO Reg. 41, FS Code)
MTR: Mass Transit Railway Corporation — TOD developments and railway protection
MW01: Minor works form — Class I notification before commencement
MW03: Minor works form — Class II notification before commencement
MW05: Minor works form — Class III completion notification
MWCS: Minor Works Control System under Building (Minor Works) Regulation
NEC4: New Engineering Contract fourth edition — collaborative contract suite (ECC)
NTEH: New Territories Exempted House — village house policy (small house)
OC: Owners Corporation — incorporated body managing common parts under BMO Cap. 344
OFCA: Office of the Communications Authority — telecommunications licensing and undertaker regulation
OP: Occupation Permit — BD certification that building is fit for occupation
OTTV: Overall Thermal Transfer Value — facade heat gain metric (W/m²)
PC: Practical Completion — contractual milestone; risk often transfers to employer
PCI: Pre-construction information — H&S and site information pack for contractor mobilisation
PC Sum: Prime Cost Sum — provisional sum for nominated sub-contract work
PD: Principal Datum — Hong Kong height reference system (mPD)
PI: Professional Indemnity insurance — design liability cover
PlanD: Planning Department — OZP, planning applications under Town Planning Ordinance
PNAP: Practice Notes for Authorized Persons, Registered Structural Engineers and Registered Geotechnical Engineers
PPE: Place of Public Entertainment — assembly occupancy under FS Code / licensing
PR: Plot Ratio — permissible GFA divided by site area per OZP/lease
QS: Quantity Surveyor — cost planning, BoQ, valuations, final account
RC: Registered Contractor — BO contractor registration for building works
RGE: Registered Geotechnical Engineer — geotechnical design and certification
RMWC: Registered Minor Works Contractor — MWCS contractor registration
RSE: Registered Structural Engineer — structural design and certification
RTTV: Roof Thermal Transfer Value — roof heat gain metric (W/m²)
RV: Rateable Value — basis for some government fees and charges
SFBC: Standard Form of Building Contract — HKIA/HKIS/HKICM private sector forms
SUC: Code of Practice for Structural Use of Concrete 2013
SUS: Code of Practice for Structural Use of Steel 2011
T8: Typhoon signal No. 8 — inclement weather stand-down in HK contracts
TD: Transport Department — traffic impact assessment and temporary traffic management on public roads
TIA: Traffic Impact Assessment — planning-stage traffic study, often a s.16 condition
TMP: Temporary Traffic Management Plan — construction-phase traffic accommodation on public roads
TPB: Town Planning Board — decides planning applications and plan amendments
TPO: Town Planning Ordinance Cap. 131 — planning permission framework
TPO s.12A: Application for review of planning decision
UBW: Unauthorized Building Works — works not approved under BO; enforcement risk
URA: Urban Renewal Authority — redevelopment and compensation frameworks
VM: Value Management — structured workshop to optimize scope, cost, programme
WSD: Water Supplies Department — plumbing, fire mains, WWO 46 inspection
假設工程: Site establishment works — hoarding, utilities, survey, mobilisation before main construction
